blocks:
Sūrat Maryam

Meccan, and it is 98 verses

(١) كهيعص (1) Kãf Hã’ Yã’ ‘Ayn Sãd.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from the Proof, the Riser (الحجّة القائم) (peace be upon him), in a hadith: that He was asked about its interpretation, and He said: "These letters are from the tidings (أنباء) of the Unseen (الغيب) which Allah informed His servant Zachariah (زكريّا) of, and then He recounted them to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. And that was because Zachariah (زكريّا) asked his Lord to teach him the names of the Five (الخمسة). So Allah sent down Gabriel (جبرئيل) to him and taught them to him. Whenever Zachariah (زكريا) mentioned Muhammad, Ali, Fatimah, and Al-Hasan (peace be upon them), his sorrow was relieved and his distress was cleared. But when He mentioned Al-Husayn (peace be upon him), the tears choked him and the gasp (البهرة) overcame him. So He said one day: 'My God, what is the matter with me that when I mention four of them, I am consoled from my sorrows by their names, but when I mention Al-Husayn (peace be upon him), my eye tears up and my sigh (زفرتي) rises?' So He, the Blessed and Exalted, informed him of His story, and He said: كهيعص ** So the Kāf (الكاف) is the name of Karbala (كربلاء), and the Hā’ (الهاء) is the destruction (هلاك) of the Progeny (العترة), and the Yā’ (الياء) is Yazid (may Allah curse him), and He is the oppressor of Al-Husayn (peace be upon him), and the ‘Ayn (العين) is His thirst (عطشه), and the Sād (الصّاد) is His patience (صبره). When Zachariah (peace be upon him) heard that, He did not leave His mosque for three days, and He prevented people from entering upon Him during that time, and He devoted Himself to weeping and wailing (النّحيب). And His lamentation (ندبته) was: 'My God, will You afflict the best of Your creation with grief over His son? Will You bring down the calamity (بلوى) of this progeny (الزرّية) upon His courtyard? My God, will You clothe Ali and Fatimah (peace be upon them) in the garments of this tragedy? My God, will You unleash the distress (كرب) of this disaster upon their domain?' Then He would say: 'My God, grant me a son by whom my eye may be comforted in old age, and make Him an heir (وارثاً) and a successor (وصيّاً), and make His status with me the status of Al-Husayn (peace be upon him). And when You grant Him to me, test me with His love, and then afflict me with grief over Him, just as You afflict Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, Your beloved, with grief over His son.' So Allah granted Him John (يحيى) (peace be upon him) and afflicted Him with grief over Him. And the pregnancy of John (peace be upon him) was six months, and the pregnancy of Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) was likewise."

And in al-Manaqib, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): the like of it.

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Its meaning is: I am the Sufficient (الكافي), the Guide (الهادي), the Guardian (الولي), the All-Knowing (العالم), the Truthful in Promise (الصادق الوعد).

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): Sufficient (كافٍ) for Our followers (شيعتنا), a Guide (هادٍ) for them, a Guardian (وليٌ) for them, All-Knowing (عالم) of the people of Our obedience, Truthful (صادق) in His promise to them, until He makes them reach the station (المنزلة) which He promised them in the core (بطن) of the Quran.

And Al-Qummi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): These are the names of Allah, cut short (مقطّعة). Then He mentioned something close to what preceded.

And in al-Majma‘, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): that He said in His supplication (دعائه): O Kāf Hā’ Yā’ ‘Ayn Sād (يا كهيعص).

(٢) ذِكْرُ رَحْمَتِ رَبِّكَ عَبْدَهُ زَكَرِيَّا ** (2) (This is) a mention of the mercy of your Lord to His servant Zechariah. Meaning, this is ذِكْرُ رَحْمَتِ رَبِّكَ. (a mention of the mercy of your Lord). In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): ذِكْرُ رَحْمَتِ رَبِّكَ عَبْدَهُ زَكَرِيَّا ** (a mention of the mercy of your Lord to His servant Zechariah), so He had mercy upon him. (٣) إِذْ نادى رَبَّهُ نِداءً خَفِيًّا ** (3) when he called out to his Lord a secret call. Perhaps that was because it is more submissive (إخباتاً) and more sincere (إخلاصاً). In al-Majma', in the Hadith: The best supplication (الدّعاء) is the secret one (الخفي) and the best provision (الرّزق) is that which suffices. (٤) قالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي وَهَنَ الْعَظْمُ مِنِّي ** (4) He said, 'My Lord! The bones in me have become feeble, and my head is glowing with hoariness. Al-Qummi says: it means weakness (ضعف). وَاشْتَعَلَ الرَّأْسُ شَيْباً ** (and my head is glowing with hoariness). He likened the hoariness, in its whiteness and illumination, to the flame (شواظ) of fire, and its spreading throughout the hair to the fire's kindling (اشتعال). In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): People did not used to turn gray (يشيبون). Then Abraham (إبراهيم) saw grayness (شيباً) in his beard and said: "O Lord, what is this?" He said: "This is dignity (وقار)." He said: "O Lord, increase me in dignity (وقاراً)." وَلَمْ أَكُنْ بِدُعائِكَ رَبِّ شَقِيًّا ** and I have never been disappointed in my prayer to You, my Lord! Rather, whenever I called upon You, You responded to me. And this is seeking nearness (توسّل) through the response (الاستجابة) that preceded for him, and an alert that even if the thing prayed for (المدعوّ له) was not customary, His response (إجابته) is customary, and that He, the Exalted, accustomed him to the response (الاجابة) and gave him hope in it. And it is the right of the Generous One (الكريم) not to disappoint the one whom He has given hope.

(٥) وَإِنِّي خِفْتُ الْمَوالِيَ مِنْ وَرائِي ** (5) 'Indeed, I fear my kinsmen after me,' after my death, that they will not manage my succession (خلافتي) well over my community (أمّتي) and they will change their religion for them. And it was read with al-qasr (بالقَصر) and the yā' (الياء) having a fathah (فتح). In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are the paternal uncles (العمومة) and the paternal cousins (بنو العمّ). And al-Qummi says: I feared the heirs (الورثة) after me. And in al-Jawami': Al-Sajjad and al-Baqir (peace be upon them both) read خفّت (with fathah on the khā', tashdīd on the fā', and kasrah on the tā'), meaning they were few (قلّوا) and incapable (عجزوا) of establishing the religion after me. وَكانَتِ امْرَأَتِي عاقِراً ** (5) 'and my wife is barren.' She does not give birth. فَهَبْ لِي مِنْ لَدُنْكَ ** (5) 'So grant me from Yourself' a mercy (رحمة), for the like of this is not hoped for except from Your favor (فضلك) and the perfection of Your power (كمال قدرتك). وَلِيًّا ** (5) 'a successor,' from my loins (صُلبي).

(٦) يَرِثُنِي وَيَرِثُ مِنْ آلِ يَعْقُوبَ ** (6) He shall inherit me and inherit from the progeny of Jacob And it was recited with the jasm (بالجزم). In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sajjad and al-Baqir (peace be upon them both): They both recited it as يَرِثُنِي وَأَرِثُ مِنْ آلِ يَعْقُوبَ (He shall inherit me and I shall inherit from the progeny of Jacob). وَاجْعَلْهُ رَبِّ رَضِيًّا ** and make him, my Lord, pleasing [to You] Whom You are pleased with in word and deed. Al-Qummi: At that time, Zakariyya did not have a child who could stand in his place and inherit him. And the gifts and vows of the Children of Israel were for the priests (الأحبار), and Zakariyya was the chief of the priests (رئيس الأحبار). And Zakariyya's wife was the sister of Maryam, daughter of 'Imran ibn Matan and Ya'qub ibn Matan. And the sons of Matan at that time were the chiefs (رؤساء) of the Children of Israel and the sons of their kings, and they were from the progeny of Sulayman ibn Dawud.

(٧) يا زَكَرِيَّا إِنَّا نُبَشِّرُكَ بِغُلامٍ اسْمُهُ يَحْيى ** (7) (O Zachariah! We give you the good news of a son whose name is John.) (This is) a response to his call and a promise to answer his supplication. And He only took charge of naming him as an honor for him. لَمْ نَجْعَلْ لَهُ مِنْ قَبْلُ سَمِيًّا ** (We have not made anyone before him with the same name.) القمّيّ says: No one before him was named with the name Yahya.

قالَ رَبِّ أَنَّى يَكُونُ لِي غُلامٌ وَكانَتِ امْرَأَتِي عاقِراً وَقَدْ بَلَغْتُ مِنَ الْكِبَرِ عِتِيًّا ٨ (He said, 'My Lord, how shall I have a son, when my wife is barren, and I have reached a prodigious old age?')

(The word عِتِيًّا) is derived from عتا الشّيخ يعتو when the old man grew old and aged, and its origin is عُتُوًّا. He only expressed astonishment at the child (being born) from an old, decrepit man (شيخ فان) and a barren old woman (عجوز عاقر) in recognition that the effective cause (المؤثّر) in it is the perfection of His power (كمال قدرته), and that the means (الوسائط) are, upon verification (عند التّحقيق), rendered null (ملغاة).

In al-Kafi, narrated from them (the Imams) (peace be upon them): Regarding what Allah admonished Jesus (peace be upon him) with: "And your counterpart, John (يحيى), among My creation, I bestowed Him upon His mother after old age, without strength (قوّة) being in her. I intended by that that My sovereignty (سُلطاني) should become manifest to her, and My power (قدرتي) should become manifest in you."

(٩) قالَ ** (He said)—meaning Allah or the angel giving the glad tidings. كَذلِكَ ** (Thus)—meaning the matter is thus, or it is in the accusative case (منصوب) due to qāla (said) in قالَ رَبُّكَ ** (Your Lord said). And that (ذلك) is a reference to something ambiguous (مبهم) which is explained by: هُوَ عَلَيَّ هَيِّنٌ وَقَدْ خَلَقْتُكَ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَلَمْ تَكُ شَيْئاً (it is easy for Me, and I created you before when you were nothing) rather, you were pure non-existence (معدوماً صرفاً).

(١٠) قالَ رَبِّ اجْعَلْ لِي آيَةً ** (He said, 'My Lord, appoint a sign for me.') a sign (علامة) by which I may know the occurrence of what You have given me glad tidings of. قالَ آيَتُكَ أَلَّا (He said, 'Your sign is that you will not) تُكَلِّمَ النَّاسَ ثَلاثَ لَيالٍ سَوِيًّا ** (be able to speak to the people for three nights, while being sound.') the creation is sound (سَوِيًّا); there is no dumbness (خرس) or muteness (بكم) in you. And in Surah Aal Imran: ثَلاثَةَ أَيَّامٍ (three days). And in it is an indication that he devoted himself to remembrance (الذّكر) and gratitude (الشكر) for three days with their nights.

(١١) فَخَرَجَ عَلى قَوْمِهِ مِنَ الْمِحْرابِ ** (11) Then he emerged before his people from the sanctuary, from the place of prayer (المصلّى) or from the chamber (الغرفة). فَأَوْحى إِلَيْهِمْ ** So he signaled (أومى) to them, due to His saying: إِلَّا رَمْزاً أَنْ سَبِّحُوا ** except by a gesture: 'Glorify [Him],' Pray (صلّوا) or declare the transcendence (نزّهوا) of your Lord, morning (بُكْرَةً) and evening (عَشِيًّا), the two ends (طرفي) of the day. And perhaps he was commanded to glorify (Him) (يسبّح) and to command his people to agree with him.

(١٢) يا يَحْيى ** upon the estimation of the saying خُذِ الْكِتابَ ** (Take the Book)—meaning the Torah—بِقُوَّةٍ (with strength), meaning with seriousness (بجدّ) and seeking support (واستظهار) through divine success (بالتوفيق).

وَآتَيْناهُ الْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّا (and We gave him judgment while a child)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Zechariah died, and his son John inherited the Book and wisdom (الحكمة) from him while he was a small child. Then He recited this Ayah.

And narrated from al-Jawad (peace be upon him): Allah argued for the Imamate with the same argument He used for prophethood, so He said: وَآتَيْناهُ الْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّا

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): The children said to John (peace be upon him), "Come, let us go play." So he said, "We were not created for play." Allah the Almighty said:

وَآتَيْناهُ الْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّا. (and We gave him judgment while a child.)

(١٣) وَحَناناً مِنْ لَدُنَّا ** (13) And a compassion from Us, And a mercy from Us upon him, and an affection. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked what was meant by His saying concerning Yahya (John): وَحَناناً مِنْ لَدُنَّا (And a compassion from Us). He said: The affection (تحنّن) of Allah. He was asked: And what extent did the affection (تحنّن) of Allah reach upon him? He said: When he would say, "O Lord," Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, would say, "I am here (لبّيك), O Yahya." And in al-Majma': That which is in its meaning. And in al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) concerning this Ayah: That when he would say in his supplication, "O Lord, O Allah," Allah would call out to him from the heaven, "I am here (لبّيك), O Yahya, ask for whatever is your need." وَزَكاةً and purity وَكانَ تَقِيًّا.** and purification, and he was Godwary.

(١٤) وَبَرًّا بِوالِدَيْهِ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ جَبَّاراً عَصِيًّا. (14) and dutiful to his parents, and he was not an arrogant rebel.)

In Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him), in Surat al-Baqarah, when interpreting His saying, the Almighty: وَاسْتَشْهِدُوا شَهِيدَيْنِ مِنْ رِجالِكُمْ (And call to witness two witnesses from among your men), Allah did not join a child (صبيّاً) with men of complete intellect (كاملي العُقول) except these four: Jesus son of Mary, John son of Zechariah, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace be upon them both and upon them all). Then He mentioned their story, and in the story of John (يحيى), He mentioned His saying, the Almighty: وَآتَيْناهُ الْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّا ** (And We gave him judgment while a child) He said: And part of that judgment (الحكم) was that when he was a child (صبيّاً), the children said to him: "Come, let us play." He said: "By Allah, we were not created for play; rather, we were created for seriousness (الجد) for a great matter." Then He said: وَحَناناً مِنْ لَدُنَّا ** (and a compassion from Us) meaning: tenderness (تحنّناً) and mercy (رحمة) upon his parents and the rest of Our servants. وَزَكاةً (and purity), meaning: purity (طهارة) for whoever believed in him and affirmed him. وَكانَ تَقِيًّا (and he was Godwary), guarding himself against evils (الشّرور) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي). وَبَرًّا بِوالِدَيْهِ (and dutiful to his parents), being kind (محسناً) to them and obedient (مطيعاً) to them. وَلَمْ يَكُنْ جَبَّاراً عَصِيًّا (and he was not an arrogant rebel), killing out of anger and striking out of anger. However, there is no servant of Allah, the Almighty, who has not erred or intended a sin (همّ بخطيئة), except for John son of Zechariah (يحيى بن زكريّا), for he neither sinned nor intended a sin (همّ بذنب).

(15) وَسَلامٌ عَلَيْهِ يَوْمَ وُلِدَ (15) Peace be upon him the day he was born

from the devil reaching him with what he reaches the children of Adam, وَيَوْمَ يَمُوتُ (and the day he dies) from the punishment of the grave (عذاب القبر), وَيَوْمَ يُبْعَثُ حَيًّا (and the day he is raised alive) from the terror of the Resurrection (هول القيامة) and the punishment of the Fire (عذاب النار).

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Verily, the most terrifying state this creation is in is in three situations (مواطن): the day he is born, and the day he exits his mother's belly and sees the world, and he dies, so he witnesses the Hereafter (الآخرة) and its people, and the day he is resurrected, so he sees judgments (احكاماً) he did not see in the abode of the world (دار الدنيا). And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), bestowed peace upon Yahya in these three situations (المواطن) and secured his fear (روعته), so He said (and He recited the Ayah). He (peace be upon him) said: And Isa son of Maryam bestowed peace upon himself in these three situations (المواطن), so he said (and he recited the coming Ayah).

(١٦) وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتابِ مَرْيَمَ إِذِ انْتَبَذَتْ مِنْ أَهْلِها مَكاناً شَرْقِيًّا (١٦) (And mention Mary in the Book, when she withdrew from her family to an eastern place) وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتابِ ** in the Quran مَرْيَمَ ** her story إِذِ انْتَبَذَتْ ** she withdrew مِنْ أَهْلِها مَكاناً شَرْقِيًّا. Al-Qummi said: She went out to the dry palm tree. I say: And its explanation will come. (١٧) فَاتَّخَذَتْ مِنْ دُونِهِمْ حِجاباً فَأَرْسَلْنا إِلَيْها رُوحَنا فَتَمَثَّلَ لَها بَشَراً سَوِيًّا (١٧) (and took a curtain apart from them. Then We sent to her Our Spirit, and He appeared to her as a well-formed human.) فَاتَّخَذَتْ مِنْ دُونِهِمْ حِجاباً ** a screen and a barrier. Al-Qummi said: in her prayer niche (محراب). فَأَرْسَلْنا إِلَيْها رُوحَنا He said: meaning Gabriel (جبرئيل) فَتَمَثَّلَ لَها بَشَراً سَوِيًّا ** It was said: in the form of a youth of sound creation. (١٨) قالَتْ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِالرَّحْمنِ مِنْكَ إِنْ كُنْتَ تَقِيًّا (١٨) (She said, 'I seek the protection of the All-beneficent from you, should you be Godwary.') قالَتْ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِالرَّحْمنِ مِنْكَ ** due to the extremity of her chastity إِنْ كُنْتَ تَقِيًّا ** you fear Allah and you heed the seeking of refuge (الاستعاذة). And the apodosis (جواب الشرط) is omitted, indicated by what preceded it, meaning: then do not expose yourself to me and be admonished by my seeking of refuge (تعويذي). Or it is related to أعوذ (I seek refuge), in which case it signifies emphasis (مبالغة).

footnote (١) Meaning **سَلامٌ عَلَيْهِ ** (peace be upon him) from us in these days.

(١٩) قالَ إِنَّما أَنَا رَسُولُ رَبِّكِ ** (19) He said, 'I am only a messenger of your Lord, الذي استعذت به لِأَهَبَ لَكِ غُلاماً ** to give you a pure son.' Whom you sought refuge in, (meaning) that I may be a cause for His gift (of the son) by breathing into the garment (الدّرع). And it was recited (لهيب) with the yāʾ (الياء). زَكِيًّا طاهراً من الذنوب أو نامياً على الخير. 'pure' (زَكِيًّا), (meaning) pure (طاهراً) from sins, or growing (نامياً) upon goodness. (٢٠) قالَتْ أَنَّى يَكُونُ لِي غُلامٌ وَلَمْ يَمْسَسْنِي بَشَرٌ ** (20) She said, 'How shall I have a son, when no human has touched me, ولم يباشرني رجل بالحلال فانّ هذه الكنايات انّما تطلق فيه وَلَمْ أَكُ بَغِيًّا ** nor have I been unchaste?' and no man has approached me through lawful means (بالحلال), for these euphemisms (الكنايات) are only applied to this (act). زانية. (Meaning) an adulteress (زانية). (٢١) قالَ كَذلِكِ قالَ رَبُّكِ هُوَ عَلَيَّ هَيِّنٌ وَلِنَجْعَلَهُ ** (21) He said, 'So shall it be. Your Lord says, "It is simple for Me, and that We may make him (Meaning) that is, We shall do that, and thus We shall make him, or that We may manifest Our power (قدرتنا) through him, and that We may make him آيَةً لِلنَّاسِ ** a sign for mankind علامة لهم وبرهاناً على كمال قدرتنا وَرَحْمَةً مِنَّا ** and a mercy from Us, a sign (علامة) for them and a proof (برهاناً) of the perfection of Our power (قدرتنا). على العباد يهتدون بإرشاده وَكانَ أَمْراً مَقْضِيًّا ** and it is a matter decided."' (Meaning) upon the servants (العباد), who will be guided by his guidance (إرشاده). تعلّق به قضاء الله في الأزل. (Meaning) to which the decree (قضاء) of Allah was attached in eternity (الأزل).

(٢٢) فَحَمَلَتْهُ ** (22) So she conceived him by the breath being blown into the opening (جيب) of her woolen garment (مدرعتها) (1), and the breath entered her womb (جوفها). Al-Qummi said: So He breathed into her opening (جيبها), and she conceived Jesus (peace be upon him) at night, and she gave birth to Him in the morning. Her pregnancy lasted nine hours; Allah made the months into hours for her. And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He took hold of the opening (جيب) of her woolen garment (مدرعتها) and breathed into it a breath, and the child was completed in the womb immediately (من ساعته), just as a child is completed in the wombs of women over nine months. Then she came out of the bathing place (المستحمّ) while she was heavily pregnant (حامل مثقل). Her aunt looked at her and disbelieved it (فأنكرتها), and Maryam went on her way, feeling shy (مستحيية) of her aunt and of Zakariyya. And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The duration of her pregnancy was nine hours. And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Maryam conceived Jesus for nine hours, each hour being a month. I say: Meaning: equivalent to a month (بمنزلة شهر). فَانْتَبَذَتْ بِهِ ** (22) and withdrew with him meaning: she secluded herself while He was in her womb, مَكاناً قَصِيًّا ** (22) to a distant place. far from her family. In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): She departed from Damascus until she reached Karbala, and she gave birth to Him in the place of the grave of al-Husayn (peace be upon him), then she returned that very night.

(1). The مدرعة (al-mudra'ah)—like (مَكْنَسَة)—is a garment like the دراعة (al-dirā'ah), and it is only made of wool (صوف).

(٢٣) فَأَجاءَهَا الْمَخاضُ ** (23) Then the birth pangs brought her The birth pangs (المخاض) brought her (فَأَجاءَهَا). This verb is originally derived from (جاء) (to come), but its usage has become specialized, similar to how (أَتَى) (to come) is specialized in (أَعْطَى) (to give). And the woman is said to be in labor (مَخَضَتْ) when the child moves in her womb, preparing to exit. إِلى جِذْعِ النَّخْلَةِ to the trunk of the palm tree so that she might conceal herself with it and lean upon it during childbirth. And it (جِذْعِ النَّخْلَةِ) is what is between the root (العِرْق) and the branch (الغُصْن). قالَتْ يا لَيْتَنِي مِتُ ** She said, 'I wish I had died And it was recited (مِتُّ) with a dammah on the mīm. قَبْلَ هذا ** before all this out of shyness (استحياء) before the people and fear of their blame.

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Because she did not see among her people any mature person (رَشِيدًا) possessing insight (فِرَاسَة) who would absolve her of evil. وَكُنْتُ نَسْياً ** and become a forgotten thing.' That which is destined to be forgotten and not sought after. And it was recited (نَسْيًا) with a fathah (on the nūn), which is a dialectical variant (لُغَة) of it, or it is a verbal noun (مَصْدَر) used to denote the passive participle (مَنْسِيًّا), meaning: forgotten in memory (مُنَسَّى الذِّكْر) such that it does not even cross their minds.

(24) فَناداها مِنْ تَحْتِها ** (24) Then he called out to her from below her, (It was) Jesus عليه السلام or Gabriel. And (مِنْ) was read with a kasrah. أَلَّا تَحْزَنِي قَدْ جَعَلَ رَبُّكِ تَحْتَكِ سَرِيًّا ** not to grieve. Your Lord has placed a rivulet below you. (Meaning) a stream (جدولاً). Thus it is mentioned in al-Jawami' narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(25) And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: Jesus struck (the ground) with his feet, and a flowing spring of water appeared. وَهُزِّي إِلَيْكِ بِجِذْعِ النَّخْلَةِ ** And shake the trunk of the palm tree toward yourself (Meaning) incline it toward yourself. تُساقِطْ عَلَيْكِ رُطَباً جَنِيًّا ** so that it lets fall upon you fresh dates. (Meaning) fresh (طريّاً). And it was read with the takhfif (lightening) of the sin and with the dammah on the ta' along with it and the kasrah on the qaf.

Al-Qummi (said): That day was a market day, and the weavers (الحاكة) met her. Weaving (الحياكة) was the noblest craft in that time. They approached her riding gray mules. Maryam said to them: "Where is the dry palm tree?" They mocked her and rebuked her. She said to them: "May Allah make your earnings meager (نزراً) and make you a disgrace (عاراً) among people." Then a group of merchants met her, and they guided her to the dry palm tree. She said to them: "May Allah place blessing (البركة) in your earnings and make people dependent on you." When she reached the palm tree, labor pains seized her, and she gave birth to Jesus عليه السلام. When she looked at him, she said: يا لَيْتَنِي مِتُّ قَبْلَ هذا وَكُنْتُ نَسْياً مَنْسِيًّا (O, would that I had died before this, and become a forgotten thing, totally forgotten!). "What shall I say to my maternal uncle, and what shall I say to the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل)?" فَناداها —Jesus—مِنْ تَحْتِها أَلَّا تَحْزَنِي قَدْ جَعَلَ رَبُّكِ تَحْتَكِ سَرِيًّا ** Then he called out to her from below her, not to grieve. Your Lord has placed a rivulet below you. (Meaning) a river (نهراً). وَهُزِّي إِلَيْكِ بِجِذْعِ النَّخْلَةِ ** And shake the trunk of the palm tree toward yourself, (Meaning) move the palm tree. تُساقِطْ عَلَيْكِ رُطَباً جَنِيًّا so that it lets fall upon you fresh dates. (Meaning) fresh (طريّا). The palm tree had been dry for a long time (منذ دهرٍ). So she extended her hands toward the palm tree, and it became leafy (أورقت) and bore fruit (أثمرت), and the fresh dates fell upon her. Her soul was comforted. Then Jesus عليه السلام said to her: "Swaddle me (قمّطيني) and arrange me (وسوّيني), then do such and such." So she swaddled Him and arranged Him.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He was walking through the gardens (بساتين) of Kufa (الكوفة) until He reached a palm tree. He performed ablution (توضأ) near it, then He bowed (ركع) and prostrated (سجد). Five hundred glorifications (تسبيحة) were counted in His prostration. Then He leaned against the palm tree and offered several supplications (دعوات). Then He said: "By Allah, this is the palm tree about which Allah, majestic is His remembrance, said to Maryam: وَهُزِّي إِلَيْكِ (And shake toward yourself...) and the rest of the Ayah."

(٢٦) فَكُلِي وَاشْرَبِي ** (26) So eat and drink of the ripe dates (الرّطب) and the water of the stream (ماء السّريّ). وَقَرِّي عَيْناً ** and be comforted. and comfort your soul (طيبي نفسك) and cast away from it that which grieved you (ارفضي عنها ما أحزنك). فَإِمَّا تَرَيِنَّ مِنَ الْبَشَرِ أَحَداً فَقُولِي إِنِّي نَذَرْتُ لِلرَّحْمنِ صَوْماً ** And if you see any human, say: ‘I have vowed a fast to the All-beneficent, meaning: silence (صمتاً). In al-Qummi: Jesus (peace be upon him) said to her: فَكُلِي وَاشْرَبِي وَقَرِّي عَيْناً فَإِمَّا تَرَيِنَّ مِنَ الْبَشَرِ أَحَداً فَقُولِي إِنِّي نَذَرْتُ لِلرَّحْمنِ صَوْماً ** (So eat and drink, and be comforted. And if you see any human, say: ‘I have vowed a fast to the All-beneficent,) and silence (وصمتاً). Thus was it revealed (كذا نزلت). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, fasting (الصيام) is not only from food and drink alone. Then He said: Maryam said: إِنِّي نَذَرْتُ لِلرَّحْمنِ صَوْماً ** (I have vowed a fast to the All-beneficent,) meaning: silence (أي صمتاً). So, when you fast, guard your tongues and lower your gazes (وغضوا أبصاركم)—the rest of the narration (الحديث). فَلَنْ أُكَلِّمَ الْيَوْمَ إِنْسِيًّا ** so I will not speak to any human today.’ And perhaps this was due to the dislike of argumentation (المجادلة) and the sufficiency of the speech of Jesus (peace be upon him), for it is decisive in silencing the slanderer (في قطع الطّاعن).

(٢٧) فَأَتَتْ بِهِ قَوْمَها تَحْمِلُهُ قالُوا يا مَرْيَمُ لَقَدْ جِئْتِ شَيْئاً فَرِيًّا ** (27) So she brought him to her people, carrying him. They said, 'O Mary, you have certainly come up with a monstrous thing!' A wondrous (بديعاً), abominable (منكراً) thing.

Al-Qummi (said): So they missed her in the prayer niche (المحراب), and they went out seeking her. And her maternal uncle, Zakariyya, went out. Then she approached while he (the child) was on her chest, and the believing women of the Children of Israel approached, spitting in her face. But she did not speak to them until she entered her prayer niche (المحراب). Then the Children of Israel and Zakariyya came to her, and they said to her, "O Mary, لَقَدْ جِئْتِ شَيْئاً فَرِيًّا. (you have certainly come up with a monstrous thing!)"

(٢٨) يا أُخْتَ هارُونَ. (28) O sister of Aaron! In al-Majma', narrated from al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah, raised (مرفوعاً) to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Aaron (هارون) was a righteous man among the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل), and everyone known for righteousness was attributed to him.

And in Sa'd al-Su'ud by Ibn Tawus (may Allah have mercy on him), narrated from him raised (مرفوعاً): The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sent him to Najran, and they said: "Do you not recite **يا أُخْتَ هارُونَ ** (O sister of Aaron!), yet there is such and such (a long period) between them?" So he mentioned that to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and He said: "Why did you not say that they used to name themselves after their prophets and the righteous among them?"

And al-Qummi: Aaron (هارون) was a wicked (فاسقاً) man, an adulterer (زانياً), so they likened her to him. ما كانَ أَبُوكِ امْرَأَ سَوْءٍ وَما كانَتْ أُمُّكِ بَغِيًّا. (Your father was not a man of evil, nor was your mother unchaste.)

(٢٩) فَأَشارَتْ إِلَيْهِ ** (29) Then she pointed to him (to Jesus (peace be upon him), meaning: Speak to him so that he may answer you). قالُوا كَيْفَ نُكَلِّمُ مَنْ كانَ They said, 'How shall we speak to someone who is فِي الْمَهْدِ صَبِيًّا. a child in the cradle?' (٣٠) قالَ إِنِّي عَبْدُ اللهِ آتانِيَ الْكِتابَ ** (30) He said, 'Indeed, I am a servant of Allah! He has given me the Book (the Gospel) وَجَعَلَنِي نَبِيًّا. and made me a prophet. (٣١) وَجَعَلَنِي مُبارَكاً أَيْنَ ما كُنْتُ. (31) He has made me blessed, wherever I may be, In al-Kafi and al-Ma'ani, and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: "Beneficial (نفّاعاً)." And in al-Kafi, narrated from them (peace be upon them), regarding what Allah admonished Jesus (peace be upon him) with: "So you were blessed as an adult and you were blessed as a child, wherever you were. I bear witness that you are My servant, the son of My maidservant." And in it (al-Kafi), narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked: "Was Jesus son of Mary, when he spoke in the cradle, Allah's proof (حجّة الله) against the people of his time?" He replied: "He was on that day a prophet, a proof (حجّة) for Allah, but not sent [as a Messenger]. Do you not hear His saying when He said: إِنِّي عَبْدُ اللهِ آتانِيَ الْكِتابَ (Indeed, I am a servant of Allah! He has given me the Book) and the rest of the Ayah?" It was asked: "Was he on that day a proof (حجّة) for Allah against Zachariah (زكريّا) in that state while he was in the cradle?" He replied: "Jesus (peace be upon him) in that state was a sign (آية) for the people and a mercy from Allah for Mary when he spoke, thus expressing on her behalf. And he was a prophet, a proof (حجّة) against whoever heard his speech in that state. Then he fell silent and did not speak until two years had passed for him. And Zachariah (زكريّا) was the proof (الحجّة) for Allah the Almighty after Jesus (peace be upon him) fell silent by two years. Then Zachariah (زكريّا) died, and his son John (يحيى) inherited the Book and wisdom from him while he was a small child. Do you not hear the saying of Him, the Mighty and Majestic: يا يَحْيى خُذِ الْكِتابَ بِقُوَّةٍ وَآتَيْناهُ الْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّا ** O John! Take the Book with power! and We gave him judgment while still a child When Jesus (peace be upon him) reached seven years of age, he spoke with prophethood (النبوّة) and messengership (الرّسالة) when Allah revealed to Him. Thus, Jesus (peace be upon him) was the proof (الحجّة) against John (يحيى) and against all people." (The rest of the narrative). And narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Jesus (peace be upon him) established the proof (بالحجّة) when he was three years old. وَأَوْصانِي بِالصَّلاةِ وَالزَّكاةِ ما دُمْتُ حَيًّا. and He has enjoined me to [maintain] the prayer and to give the zakat as long as I live, Al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), said: "The Zakat of the heads (زكاة الرّؤوس), because not all people possess wealth. Rather, the Fitrah (الفطرة) is incumbent upon the poor and the rich, the young and the old." وَبَرًّا بِوالِدَتِي ** and to be good to my mother, (and dutiful to her, being grammatically connected (عطف) to blessed) وَلَمْ يَجْعَلْنِي جَبَّاراً شَقِيًّا and He has not made me an arrogant wretch. In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He counted disobedience to parents (العقوق) among the major sins (الكبائر). He said: "Because Allah made the disobedient person (العاق) an arrogant wretch (جبّاراً شقيّاً) in His saying, relating the account of Jesus (peace be upon him): وَبَرًّا بِوالِدَتِي وَلَمْ يَجْعَلْنِي جَبَّاراً شَقِيًّا. and to be good to my mother, and He has not made me an arrogant wretch."

(٣٣) وَالسَّلامُ عَلَيَّ يَوْمَ وُلِدْتُ وَيَوْمَ أَمُوتُ وَيَوْمَ أُبْعَثُ حَيًّا ** (33) 'Peace be upon me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I am raised alive.' just as it is upon Yahya (peace be upon him). (٣٤) ذلِكَ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ ** (34) That is Jesus, son of Mary— not what the Christians describe Him as. And this is a refutation (تكذيب) of them regarding what they describe Him as, in the most eloquent manner, since He made Him (Jesus) described by the opposites (أضداد) of what they describe Him as, then He reversed the ruling. (It is) the saying of the truth (قَوْلَ الْحَقِّ), meaning: He is قَوْلَ الْحَقِّ الَّذِي ** (the saying of the truth which) there is no doubt in it. And it was recited with the nasb (النصب) (accusative case) as an emphatic infinitive (المصدر المؤكّد*): الَّذِي فِيهِ يَمْتَرُونَ. (which they doubt.) Al-Qummi said: meaning, they dispute (يتخاصمون*).

(٣٥) ما كانَ لِلَّهِ أَنْ يَتَّخِذَ مِنْ وَلَدٍ سُبْحانَهُ إِذا قَضى أَمْراً فَإِنَّما يَقُولُ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ (It is not for Allah to take a son. Immaculate is He! When He decrees a matter, He just says to it, ‘Be,’ and it is.) A refutation of the Christians and a declaration of Allah's transcendence from what they falsely accused Him of. A rebuke for them, [showing] that the One Who, if He wills a thing, brings it into existence merely by [saying] 'Be' (كُنْ), is transcendent from the likeness of creation and from the need for taking a son through the impregnation of females (باحبال الإِناث).

وَإِنَّ اللهَ رَبِّي وَرَبُّكُمْ فَاعْبُدُوهُ هذا صِراطٌ مُسْتَقِيمٌ ** (36) Indeed Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. This is a straight path. Its interpretation has preceded in Surat Āl ‘Imrān. And it was recited as inna (انّ) with a fatḥah (بالفتح), meaning: "and because" (ولأنّ) or as an ‘aṭf (عطف) (conjunction) upon al-ṣalāh (الصلاة).

فَاخْتَلَفَ الْأَحْزابُ مِنْ بَيْنِهِمْ ** (37) But the factions differed among themselves. The Jews and the Christians, or the factions (فرق) of the Christians. For among them are those who said [He is] the son of Allah, and among them are those who said He is Allah, [Who] descended to the earth then ascended to the heaven, and among them are those who said He is the servant of Allah and His Prophet. فَوَيْلٌ (١) لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ مَشْهَدِ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (1) Woe to those who disbelieve because of the presence of a tremendous day! because of witnessing a tremendous day—its terror (هوله), its reckoning (وحسابه), and its recompense (وجزاؤه).

(٣٨) أَسْمِعْ بِهِمْ وَأَبْصِرْ يَوْمَ يَأْتُونَنا (38) How well they will hear and see the day they come to Us! meaning, how well they will hear and see on the Day of Resurrection. لكِنِ الظَّالِمُونَ الْيَوْمَ فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ But today the wrongdoers are in manifest error. He used the explicit noun (الظّاهر) in the place of the implicit pronoun (المضمر), signaling that they wronged themselves when they neglected listening and seeing at the time when it would benefit them.

(٣٩) وَأَنْذِرْهُمْ يَوْمَ الْحَسْرَةِ (39) And warn them of the Day of Regret [It is] the Day when people will feel regret: the wrongdoer over his wrongdoing, and the good-doer over the scarcity of his good deeds. In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Day of Regret is the Day when death will be brought and slaughtered. إِذْ قُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ when the matter is decided [means] when the reckoning is finished and the two parties proceed to Paradise and the Fire. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and he said: A caller will call out from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and that will be after the people of Paradise have entered Paradise and the people of the Fire have entered the Fire: "O people of Paradise and O people of the Fire, do you recognize death in any form?" They will say: "No." Then death will be brought in the form of a speckled ram (كبش أملح) and stood between Paradise and the Fire. Then they will all be called: "Look up and look at death!" So they will look up. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will command it, and it will be slaughtered. Then it will be said: "O people of Paradise, [it is] eternity, so there is no death ever! And O people of the Fire, [it is] eternity, so there is no death ever!" And that is His Almighty saying: وَأَنْذِرْهُمْ يَوْمَ الْحَسْرَةِ إِذْ قُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ (And warn them of the Day of Regret when the matter is decided), meaning: Eternity in Paradise has been decreed for the people of Paradise, and eternity in the Fire has been decreed for the people of the Fire. And in al-Majma': A similar narration is reported through the way of the ‘Ammah (Sunnis) from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, except that He said: "Death will be brought as if it were a speckled ram (كبش أملح). It will be said to them: 'Do you recognize death?' They will say: 'This is it! This is it!' and everyone will have recognized it." (The Hadith). He said: And our companions (أصحابنا) narrated it from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both). Then, at the end of it, it came: "The people of Paradise will rejoice with a joy that, if anyone were to die that day, they would die from joy. And the people of the Fire will gasp a gasp that, if anyone were to die, they would die." وَهُمْ فِي غَفْلَةٍ وَهُمْ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ while they are inattentive and they do not believe. [This phrase] is related to His saying: فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ (in manifest error), and what is between them is an interjection (اعتراض); or it is related to وَأَنْذِرْهُمْ (And warn them), meaning: Warn them while they are heedless and disbelieving.

footnote (١) Meaning: the severity of punishment, and it is said: *Wayl* (*ويل*) is a valley in Hell.

(٤٠) إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَرِثُ الْأَرْضَ وَمَنْ عَلَيْها ** (40) We shall indeed inherit the earth and all those who are on it, No owner (مالك) or disposer (متصرّف) remains upon it. Al-Qummi said: Everything that Allah created, Allah inherits it on the Day of Resurrection. وَإِلَيْنا يُرْجَعُونَ ** and they shall be returned to Us. They shall be returned for the recompense (الجزاء).

(٤١) وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتابِ إِبْراهِيمَ إِنَّهُ كانَ صِدِّيقاً نَبِيًّا ** (41) And mention Ibrāhīm in the Book. Indeed, he was a truthful man, a prophet. Adhering constantly to truth (الصّدق), frequently affirming the truth (التّصديق) of the Books of Allah, His signs (آياته), and His prophets (أنبيائه), and he was a prophet in his own right.

(٤٢) إِذْ قالَ لِأَبِيهِ ** (42) When he said to his father, 'O my father! Why do you worship that which neither hears nor sees, nor can avail you anything? The discussion regarding whether he was his father, or whether he was his paternal uncle (عمه), or his maternal grandfather, has already preceded, due to the purity of the fathers of the prophets from polytheism (الشّرك). يا أَبَتِ ** The letter tā’ (التّاء) is a substitute for the yā’ of possession (ياء الإِضافة), and it is only mentioned for the purpose of seeking compassion (للاستعطاف), which is why he repeated it. لِمَ تَعْبُدُ ما لا يَسْمَعُ وَلا يُبْصِرُ ** so that it might know your condition, hear your mention, and see your submission (خضوعك). وَلا يُغْنِي عَنْكَ شَيْئاً ** in attracting benefit (نفع) or repelling harm (ضرّ).

(٤٣) يا أَبَتِ إِنِّي قَدْ جاءَنِي مِنَ الْعِلْمِ ما لَمْ يَأْتِكَ فَاتَّبِعْنِي أَهْدِكَ صِراطاً سَوِيًّا. (43) O my father! Indeed, there has come to me a knowledge that has not come to you. So follow me, and I will guide you to a straight path. (٤٤) يا أَبَتِ لا تَعْبُدِ الشَّيْطانَ إِنَّ الشَّيْطانَ كانَ لِلرَّحْمنِ عَصِيًّا (44) O my father! Do not worship Satan. Indeed, Satan has been disobedient to the All-beneficent. (٤٥) يا أَبَتِ إِنِّي أَخافُ أَنْ يَمَسَّكَ عَذابٌ مِنَ الرَّحْمنِ فَتَكُونَ لِلشَّيْطانِ وَلِيًّا ** (45) O my father! I fear that a punishment from the All-beneficent should befall you, and you become a companion of Satan. He (peace be upon him) called [him] to guidance, clarified his deviation (ضلاله), and argued against him with the most eloquent and gentle argument, with gentleness (برفق) and good manners (حسن ادب), as he did not explicitly declare his deviation (ضلاله). Instead, he inquired about the cause (العلة) that prompted him to worship that which is not worthy of worship in any respect (بوجه). Then he called him to follow him so that he might guide him to the upright truth (الحق القويم) and the straight path (الصراط المستقيم), as he was not independent in sound consideration (النظر السّويّ). He did not label him with excessive ignorance (الجهل المفرط) nor himself with superior knowledge (العلم الفائق). Rather, he made himself like a companion to him on a journey who is more knowledgeable of the path. Then he deterred him from what he was upon by [stating] that, despite being devoid of benefit (النفع), it necessitates harm (الضرّ), for it is, in reality, the worship of Satan, since he is the one who commands it. And he clarified that Satan is disobedient (مستعص) to your Lord, the Master (المولى) of all blessings (النّعم), and every disobedient one (عاص) is deserving that the blessings be withdrawn from him and that He take vengeance upon him. And for that reason, he followed it up by warning him of his evil consequence (سوء عاقبته) and what it would lead him to: becoming a close associate (قريباً) of Satan in the curse (اللّعن) and the punishment (العذاب).

(٤٦) **قالَ أَراغِبٌ أَنْتَ عَنْ آلِهَتِي يا إِبْراهِيمُ ** (46) He said, 'Are you averse to my gods, O Abraham?'

He met his plea for mercy (استعطافه) and his gentleness in guidance (لطفه في الإرشاد) with harshness (بالفظاظة) and the coarseness of obstinacy (غلظة العناد). So he called him by his name and did not respond with 'O my son' (يا بنيَّ). And he delayed it [the subject], and placed the predicate (الخبر) before the subject (المبتدأ), and began it with the interrogative hamza of denial (همزة الإنكار) coupled with a kind of astonishment (التعجّب). Then he threatened him: لَئِنْ لَمْ تَنْتَهِ ** (If you do not desist) from your speech concerning them [the gods] and being averse to them (الرّغبة عنها), لَأَرْجُمَنَّكَ ** (I will stone you) with my tongue or with stones (بالحجارة). وَاهْجُرْنِي ** (And shun me)— and beware of me (واحذرني) and shun me by going away from me (بالذّهاب عنّي)— مَلِيًّا ** (for a long time), meaning a long time (زماناً طويلاً).

(٤٧) قالَ سَلامٌ عَلَيْكَ ** (47) He said, 'Peace be upon you! [It is] a farewell, a leaving [of the matter], and a meeting of evil with good. Meaning: I will not afflict you with any harm, nor will I say anything to you after this that might hurt you. سَأَسْتَغْفِرُ لَكَ رَبِّي ** I shall plead with my Lord for your forgiveness. Perhaps He grants you success for repentance and faith. إِنَّهُ كانَ بِي حَفِيًّا ** Indeed He is gracious to me.' [Meaning: He is] profound in kindness (البرّ) and favor (الاعطاف).

(٤٨) وَأَعْتَزِلُكُمْ وَما تَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (I will withdraw from you and what you invoke besides Allah) by migrating with my religion (ديني). وَأَدْعُوا رَبِّي ** (and I will supplicate my Lord) and I will worship Him alone. عَسى أَلَّا أَكُونَ بِدُعاءِ رَبِّي شَقِيًّا ** (I hope that I will not be wretched in supplicating my Lord) disappointed (خائباً), whose effort (السّعي) is wasted, like you in supplicating your deities (آلهتكم). And in beginning the speech with (عسى) (perhaps/I hope) is humility (التّواضع) and self-effacement (هضم النفس), and an alert (التنبيه) that the response (الإجابة) and the reward (الإثابة) are a favor (تفضل), not an obligation (غير واجب), and that the essence (ملاك) of the matter is its conclusion (خاتمته), and that is the unseen (غيب).

(49) فَلَمَّا اعْتَزَلَهُمْ وَما يَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (So when he separated himself from them and what they worshipped besides Allah) by migrating to al-Sham (الشّام). وَهَبْنا لَهُ إِسْحاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ ** (We bestowed upon him Isaac and Jacob) as a replacement for the disbelievers (الكفرة) whom he separated from. وَكُلًّا جَعَلْنا نَبِيًّا (and We made each of them a prophet)

(50) وَوَهَبْنا لَهُمْ مِنْ رَحْمَتِنا وَجَعَلْنا لَهُمْ لِسانَ صِدْقٍ عَلِيًّا ** (and We bestowed on them some of Our mercy, and We appointed for them a lofty renown of truth) It was said that the mercy (الرّحمة) is prophethood (النبوّة), wealth (الأموال), and children (الأولاد), and it is general, encompassing every religious (دينيّ) and worldly (دنيويّ) good. And the 'tongue of truth' (لسان الصدق) is good praise (الثناء الحسن). He expressed by the tongue (باللّسان) what is found through it, just as He expresses by the hand (باليد) what is released by the hand, which is the gift (العطيّة). And al-ʿAliyy (العليّ) is the lofty/exalted (المرتفع). For all people of the religions (اهل الأديان) follow him, praise him and his progeny, and take pride in him. And this is an answer to his supplication where he said: وَاجْعَلْ لِي لِسانَ صِدْقٍ فِي الْآخِرِينَ** (And appoint for me a truthful renown among the posterity).

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Zakiyy عليه السلام: وَوَهَبْنا لَهُمْ ** meaning, upon Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob (peace be upon them) مِنْ رَحْمَتِنا ** [meaning] the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. وَجَعَلْنا لَهُمْ لِسانَ صِدْقٍ عَلِيًّا ** [meaning] the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq, from the Commander of the Faithful عليهما السلام: The tongue of truth (لسان الصدق) for a man, which Allah places among the people, is better than the wealth (المال) he consumes and leaves as inheritance.

(٥١) وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتابِ مُوسى إِنَّهُ كانَ مُخْلَصاً ** (51) And mention Mūsā in the Book. Indeed, he was sincere a monotheist (موحّداً) who purified his worship from polytheism (الشّرك) and hypocrisy (الرّياء) and submitted his face to Allah. And it was read with the fathah on the lām (i.e., مُخْلَصاً), meaning that Allah purified him (أخلصه الله). وَكانَ رَسُولاً نَبِيًّا ** and was a messenger, a prophet. It is said that Allah sent him to the creation, and he informed them about Him. For this reason, "messenger" (رسولاً) was placed first, even though it is more specific (أخصّ).

And upon (وعلى). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, "What is the Messenger (الرّسول) and what is the Prophet (النّبي)?" He said: "The Prophet (النّبي) is he who sees in his sleep and hears the voice but does not behold the Angel (الملك). And the Messenger (الرسول) is he who hears the voice, sees in his sleep, and beholds the Angel (الملك)."

(٥٢) وَنادَيْناهُ مِنْ جانِبِ الطُّورِ الْأَيْمَنِ وَقَرَّبْناهُ نَجِيًّا ** (52) We called him from the right side of the Mount, and brought him near for a private conversation. Conversing privately (مناجياً). A drawing near of honor (تقريب تشريف). He likened Him to someone whom the king brings near for a private conversation (لمناجاته). (٥٣) وَوَهَبْنا لَهُ مِنْ رَحْمَتِنا أَخاهُ ** (53) And out of Our mercy We gave him his brother The assistance of his brother and his support, in response to his supplication: وَاجْعَلْ لِي وَزِيراً مِنْ أَهْلِي ** (And appoint for me a minister from my family), for he (Harun) was older than Musa (peace be upon him). Harun, a prophet (نَبِيًّا). In al-Ikmal (في الإكمال): Musa (peace be upon him) lived one hundred and twenty-six years, and Harun lived one hundred and thirty-three years.

(54) وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتابِ إِسْماعِيلَ إِنَّهُ كانَ صادِقَ الْوَعْدِ وَكانَ رَسُولاً نَبِيًّا. (And mention Ismael in the Book. Indeed, he was true to his promise, and was an apostle and a prophet.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was only named **صادِقَ الْوَعْدِ ** (true to his promise) because he promised a man [to meet him] in a place, and he waited for him in that place for a year. Thus, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, named Him true to His promise. Then, that man came to him after that, and Ismael said to him: "I have not ceased waiting for you."

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): regarding its meaning. And al-Qummi said: He made a promise and waited for his companion for a year. He said: And he is Ismael son of Hizqil (إسماعيل بن حزقيل).

In al-Majma': He is Ismael son of Ibrahim, and when he promised something, he fulfilled it and did not break it. Furthermore, he was an apostle and a prophet sent to [the tribe of] Jurhum (جُرْهُم).

He said: And it was said that Ismael son of Ibrahim died before his father, and that this [prophet mentioned in the Ayah] is Ismael son of Hizqil (إسماعيل بن حزقيل). And he mentioned what follows from al-Ilal (العلل) and attributed it to al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

In al-Ilal, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: Indeed, the Ismael whom Allah mentioned in His Book, وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتابِ (And mention in the Book) [the rest of] the Ayah, was not Ismael son of Ibrahim, but rather he was a prophet among the prophets whom Allah sent to his people. So they seized him and flayed the skin of his head and his face. Then an angel came to him and said: "Indeed, Allah, Glorious is His Majesty, has sent me to you, so command me whatever you wish." He replied: "I have an example (أُسْوَة) in what is done to the prophets." And in another narration: He replied: "I have an example (أُسْوَة) in al-Husayn son of Ali (peace be upon them both)."

(٥٥) وَكانَ يَأْمُرُ أَهْلَهُ بِالصَّلاةِ وَالزَّكاةِ وَكانَ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِ مَرْضِيًّا. (55) and he used to enjoin his family to [perform] the prayer and [pay] the zakat, and he was pleasing to his Lord. (٥٦) وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتابِ إِدْرِيسَ ** (56) And mention Idris in the Book. It was said that he is the grandson (سبط) of Seth (شيث) and the grandfather of Noah's father, and his name is Enoch (أخنوخ). And it was narrated: that thirty scrolls (صحيفة) were revealed to him, and that he was the first to write with the pen (بالقلم), and to look into the science of the stars (علم النّجوم) and calculation (الحساب), and the first to sew clothes and wear them, whereas they used to wear skins (الجلود). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: And he was named Idris (إدريس) due to his frequent study of the Books (الكتب). إِنَّهُ كانَ صِدِّيقاً نَبِيًّا** He was indeed a truthful man and a prophet.

(٥٧) وَرَفَعْناهُ مَكاناً عَلِيًّا ** (And We raised him to a high station.) It was said: [This refers to] the honor of prophethood and closeness (الزّلفى) to Allah.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Gabriel informed me that a certain angel among the angels had a great status with Allah, but He became displeased with him, so He lowered him from the heaven to the earth. He came to Idris (peace be upon him) and said to him: 'You have a status with Allah, so intercede for me with your Lord.' So he prayed for three nights without ceasing and fasted during their days without breaking the fast. Then, at dawn (السّحر), he requested Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, concerning the angel. The angel said: 'Indeed, you have been granted your request, and Allah has released my wings for me. I would like to reward you, so ask me for a need.' He said: 'Show me the Angel of Death (ملك الموت), perhaps I may become familiar with him, for nothing brings me joy (يَهْنِئُنِي) while remembering him.' So he spread his wing and then said: 'Mount up.' He ascended with him and sought the Angel of Death in the lowest heaven (السّماء الدّنيا). It was said to him: 'Ascend.' So he met him between the fourth and fifth heavens. The angel said: 'O Angel of Death, why do I see you frowning (قَاطِباً)?' He replied: 'It is astonishing! I am under the shade of the Throne, where I was commanded to seize the soul of a human being between the fourth and fifth heavens.' Idris heard this, [and was distressed (فَامْتَعَضَ)], so he fell from the angel's wing, and [the Angel of Death] seized his soul right there. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَرَفَعْناهُ مَكاناً عَلِيًّا."

And al-Qummi [narrated] what is close to it.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said in a narration mentioning the Mosque of al-Sahla: "Did you not know that it is the location of the house of Idris the Prophet صلى الله عليه, where he used to sew?"

(٥٨) **أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّةِ آدَمَ وَمِمَّنْ حَمَلْنا مَعَ نُوحٍ وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّةِ إِبْراهِيمَ وَإِسْرائِيلَ وَمِمَّنْ هَدَيْنا وَاجْتَبَيْنا إِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِمْ آياتُ الرَّحْمنِ خَرُّوا سُجْداً وَبُكِيًّا ** (58) They are the ones whom Allah has blessed from among the prophets, from the progeny of Adam, and of those whom We carried with Noah, and of the progeny of Abraham and Israel, and of those whom We guided and chose. When the signs of the All-beneficent are recited to them, they fall down in prostration, weeping.

أُولئِكَ ** (Those) is a reference to those mentioned in the Surah, from Zakariyya up to Idris (peace be upon him). الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (whom Allah has blessed) with various types of religious (الدينية) and worldly (الدنياويّة) blessings, مِنْ النَّبِيِّينَ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّةِ آدَمَ وَمِمَّنْ حَمَلْنا مَعَ نُوحٍ (from among the prophets, from the progeny of Adam, and of those whom We carried with Noah)—meaning, and specifically from the progeny of those whom We carried, and they are those besides Idris, for indeed Ibrahim (peace be upon him) was from the progeny of Sam son of Noah. وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّةِ إِبْراهِيمَ ** (and of the progeny of Abraham)—the remaining ones. وَإِسْرائِيلَ ** (and Israel)—meaning, and from the progeny of Israel, and among them were Musa, Harun, Zakariyya, Yahya, and Isa (peace be upon them). And in this is an indication (دلالة) that the children of daughters are considered part of the progeny (الذّرية). وَمِمَّنْ هَدَيْنا وَاجْتَبَيْنا ** (and of those whom We guided and chose) for prophethood (النبوّة) and honor (الكرامة).

In al-Manaqib and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): "We are the ones meant by it." إِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِمْ آياتُ الرَّحْمنِ خَرُّوا سُجْداً وَبُكِيًّا ** (When the signs of the All-beneficent are recited to them, they fall down in prostration, weeping)—out of fear (خشية) of Allah and submission (اخباتاً) to Him.

It is narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny): "Recite the Quran and weep, and if you cannot weep, then pretend to weep (فتباكوا)." And البكيّ (al-bukiyy) is the plural of باك (bākin), just as السجّود (al-sujūd) is the plural of ساجد (sājid). And it was recited with a kasra on the bā' (بِكِيّ).

(٥٩) فَخَلَفَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ خَلْفٌ ** (59) Then successors followed them They succeeded them, and after them came an evil progeny (عَقْب سُوء). It is said that خَلَف صِدْق (a righteous successor) is pronounced with the fatḥah (on the khāʾ), and خَلْف سُوء (an evil successor) is pronounced with the sukūn (on the lām).

**أَضاعُوا الصَّلاةَ ** who neglected the prayer They delayed it past its time.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a ḥadīth: "It is not the case that if you hasten it slightly or delay it slightly that it will harm you, so long as you do not commit that specific neglect (الإضَاعَة), for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says concerning a people: أَضاعُوا الصَّلاةَ (who neglected the prayer), [and then the rest of] the Ayah."

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): "They neglected it by delaying it past its appointed times (مَوَاقِيتِهَا), without having abandoned it entirely."

وَاتَّبَعُوا الشَّهَواتِ and followed the desires

In al-Jawami', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "He who builds excessively (الشَّدِيد), rides what is conspicuous (المَنْظُور), and wears what is famous (المَشْهُور)."

فَسَوْفَ يَلْقَوْنَ غَيًّا ** Soon they will encounter ruin Meaning, evil (شَرّاً).

(٦٠) إِلَّا مَنْ تابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صالِحاً فَأُولئِكَ يَدْخُلُونَ الْجَنَّةَ ** (60) except him who repents, attains faith, and acts righteously. Such shall enter paradise, And it was recited (وقرئ) based on the passive construction (على البناء للمفعول) وَلا يُظْلَمُونَ شَيْئاً (and they will not be wronged in the least) (٦١) جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ الَّتِي وَعَدَ الرَّحْمنُ عِبادَهُ بِالْغَيْبِ إِنَّهُ كانَ وَعْدُهُ مَأْتِيًّا ** (61) gardens of Eden, which the All-beneficent has promised His servants in the Unseen. Indeed, His promise is bound to be fulfilled. Its promised time (أجله الموعود) comes to them, or it is from Him Who comes to it (أتى إليه) with goodness (إحساناً), meaning: fulfilled (مفعولاً), accomplished (منجزاً). (٦٢) لا يَسْمَعُونَ فِيها لَغْواً ** (62) They will not hear any vain talk therein, superfluous speech (فضول كلام) إِلَّا سَلاماً وَلَهُمْ رِزْقُهُمْ فِيها بُكْرَةً وَعَشِيًّا ** but only ‘Peace!’ And they will have their provision therein morning and evening. according to the custom of those who are blessed with comfort (المتغمّين) and the moderation (التوسط) between asceticism (الزّهادة) and desire (الرّغابة).

In al-Mahasin and Tibb al-A'immah, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that a man complained to him about the pains (الأوجاع) and indigestion (التّخم) he suffered. So he (peace be upon him) said: "Eat lunch (تغدّ) and eat dinner (تعشّ), and do not eat anything between them, for in that is the corruption of the body. Have you not heard Allah say: لَهُمْ رِزْقُهُمْ فِيها بُكْرَةً وَعَشِيًّا (And they will have their provision therein morning and evening)?" Al-Qummi said: That is in the gardens of the world (جنات الدّنيا) before the Day of Resurrection, because the morning (البكرة) and the evening (العشيّ) will not exist in the Hereafter in the Gardens of Eternity (جنّات الخلد). Rather, they exist in the gardens of the world to which the souls of the believers are transferred, and in which the sun and the moon rise.

(٦٣) تِلْكَ الْجَنَّةُ الَّتِي نُورِثُ مِنْ عِبادِنا مَنْ كانَ تَقِيًّا (63) That is the Garden which We shall bequeath to such of Our servants as are Godwary. In al-Tahdhib, concerning the supplications (أدعية) for the supererogatory prayers (نوافل) of the month of Ramadan: Glory be to Him Who created the Garden for Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad. Glory be to Him Who bequeaths it to Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad and their followers (شيعتهم).

(٦٤) وَما نَتَنَزَّلُ إِلَّا بِأَمْرِ رَبِّكَ ** A narration of the saying of Gabriel (جبرئيل). In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: that he said to Gabriel (جبرئيل): "What prevented you from visiting us?" So, the Ayah descended: لَهُ ما بَيْنَ أَيْدِينا وَما خَلْفَنا وَما بَيْنَ ذلِكَ (64) We do not descend except by the command of your Lord. To Him belongs whatever is before us and whatever is behind us and whatever is between that. And that is what we are in, concerning places (الأماكن) and times (الأحانين). We do not move from place to place, nor do we descend at one time rather than another, except by His command and His will (مشيّته). وَما كانَ رَبُّكَ نَسِيًّا ** Your Lord has not been forgetful. One Who abandons you. In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام, regarding this Ayah: "Indeed, our Lord, blessed and exalted be He, with great exaltation (علوّاً كبيراً), is not the One Who forgets (ينسى) or is heedless (يغفل). Rather, He is the Subtle (اللطيف), the Preserver (الحفيظ), the All-Knowing (العليم)."

(65) رَبُّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما بَيْنَهُما ** (The Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them.) A clarification for the impossibility of forgetfulness upon Him. فَاعْبُدْهُ وَاصْطَبِرْ لِعِبادَتِهِ ** (So worship Him and be steadfast in His worship.) An address to the Messenger, upon which is consequent [His saying]: هَلْ تَعْلَمُ لَهُ سَمِيًّا (Do you know for Him any namesake?).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Its interpretation is: Do you know anyone whose name is Allah other than Allah.

(٦٦) وَيَقُولُ الْإِنْسانُ أَإِذا ما مِتُّ لَسَوْفَ أُخْرَجُ حَيًّا ** (66) Man says, 'When I am dead, shall I be raised up alive?' Since this statement was present among their kind (جنسهم), it was attributed to the genus (الجنس). And it was narrated that Ubayy ibn Khalaf took decayed bones, crushed them, and said: "Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله claims that we shall be resurrected after we die."

(٦٧) أَوَلا يَذْكُرُ الْإِنْسانُ ** (Does not man remember) And it was read يَذَّكَّرُ (yadhdhakkar) from الذِّكْر (al-dhikr), which is intended to mean contemplation (التفكّر): أَنَّا خَلَقْناهُ مِنْ قَبْلُ (that We created him before) Meaning: We decreed him in [Our] knowledge, when Allah existed and nothing was with Him, وَلَمْ يَكُ شَيْئاً ** (and he was nothing) Rather, he was pure non-existence (عَدَماً صَرْفاً).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: "Neither decreed (مُقَدَّراً) nor formed (مُكَوَّناً)."

And in al-Mahasin, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "He was not a thing in a book nor in knowledge."

And al-Qummi [said]: Meaning, there was no mention (ذِكْرُهُ) of him there (ثَمَّة).

(٦٨) فَوَ رَبِّكَ لَنَحْشُرَنَّهُمْ وَالشَّياطِينَ ** (So, by your Lord, We will surely gather them and the devils) (ʿaṭf - coordination) or (mafʿūl maʿah - object of accompaniment), because it is narrated that the disbelievers will be gathered with their companions from the devils who misguided them, each one with his devil in a chain. ثُمَّ لَنُحْضِرَنَّهُمْ حَوْلَ جَهَنَّمَ جِثِيًّا. (then We will surely bring them present around hell, kneeling.) Al-Qummi said: on their knees. I say: And this is as is customary in situations of contention (التقاول), and it is like the Almighty's saying: وَتَرى كُلَّ أُمَّةٍ جاثِيَةً.** (And you will see every nation kneeling.)

(٦٩) ثُمَّ لَنَنْزِعَنَّ مِنْ كُلِّ شِيعَةٍ أَيُّهُمْ أَشَدُّ عَلَى الرَّحْمنِ عِتِيًّا ** (69) Then We will certainly pull out from every faction those of them who were most defiant towards the All-beneficent. From every nation (أمّة) that followed (شاعت) a religion (دينا), meaning: that followed it. Those who were the most disobedient (أعصى) and rebellious (أعتى) among them, and We shall cast them into it (Hell).

(٧٠) ثُمَّ لَنَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِالَّذِينَ هُمْ أَوْلى بِها صِلِيًّا ** (70) Then We surely know best those who are worthier of being roasted in it. Worthier of the roasting (بالصّلي).

(٧١) وَإِنْ مِنْكُمْ إِلَّا وارِدُها (71) There is none of you but shall come to it: Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: "Do you not hear a man say, 'We arrived at (وردنا) the water of the tribe of so-and-so'? That is the arrival (الورود), and he has not entered." كانَ عَلَى رَبِّكَ حَتْماً مَقْضِيًّا ** (it is) a decree which your Lord has ordained necessarily. Their arrival (ورودهم) was an obligation (واجباً) which Allah made incumbent upon Himself and decreed it.

(٧٢) ثُمَّ نُنَجِّي الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا ** (Then We shall deliver those who were Godwary) so they will be driven to Paradise (الجنّة). It was recited nunjī (ننجي) with takhfīf (التخفيف) (lightening the jīm): وَنَذَرُ الظَّالِمِينَ فِيها جِثِيًّا** (and leave the wrongdoers in it, kneeling) upon their posture as they were.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "People will approach the Fire (النّار), then they will depart according to their deeds. The first of them will be like the flash of lightning, then like the passing of the wind, then like the galloping of a horse, then like a rider, then like a man running with a load (شدّ الرّحل), then like his walking."

And narrated from him صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "The approaching (الورود) is the entering. No righteous (برّ) or wicked (فاجر) person will remain except that he enters it, but it will be cool and safe (برداً وسلاماً) for the believers, just as it was for Abraham (peace be upon him), until the Fire—or he said, Hell (جهنّم)—will have a clamor (ضجيجاً) from its coolness." ثُمَ ** (Then) Allah will deliver الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا وَنَذَرُ الظَّالِمِينَ فِيها جِثِيًّا** (those who were Godwary, and We leave the wrongdoers in it, kneeling).

And narrated from him صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "The Fire (النّار) will say to the believer on the Day of Resurrection: 'Pass quickly, O believer, for your light has extinguished my flame.'"

And in a narration (رواية): "Indeed, Allah Almighty will make the Fire (النّار) like solidified ghee (السّمن الجامد), and creation will gather upon it. Then the caller (المنادي) will call out: 'Take your companions and leave My companions.' He said: 'By Him in Whose hand is my soul, it (the Fire) is more knowledgeable of its companions than a mother is of her child.'"

It was said that the benefit (الفائدة) in that is what was narrated in some reports (الأخبار): "Indeed, Allah will not admit anyone into Paradise (الجنّة) until He informs him of the Fire (النّار) and the torment within it, so that he may know the completeness of Allah's favor upon him and the perfection of His kindness and benevolence toward him, and thus increase in joy and happiness due to Paradise and its bliss. Nor will anyone enter the Fire until He informs him of Paradise and the types of bliss and reward within it, so that this may be an increase in punishment for him and a regret (حسرة) for what he missed of Paradise and its bliss."

He said: And it has been reported in the narration (الخبر): "Indeed, fever (الحمّى) is from the pus (قيح) of Hell (جهنّم)."

And it was narrated: "Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم visited a sick person and said: 'Be glad! Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: "It is My Fire (ناري) which I unleash upon My believing servant in the world, so that it may be his share of the Fire (النّار)."'"

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Fever (الحمّى) is the scout (رائد) of death, and it is the prison of the believer on earth, and it is the believer's share (حظّ) of the Fire (النّار)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Fever (الحمّى) is the scout (رائد) of death, and the prison of Allah Almighty on His earth. Its heat (فورها) is from Hell (جهنّم), and it is the share (حظّ) of every believer from the Fire (النار)."

And in al-I'tiqadat, it was narrated: "Indeed, no pain will afflict anyone among the People of Monotheism (أهل التوحيد) in the Fire (النار) when they enter it. Rather, pain will afflict them upon exiting it, and those pains will be a recompense for what their hands have earned, وَمَا اللهُ بِظَلَّامٍ لِلْعَبِيدِ (and Allah is not tyrannical to the servants)." [End of quote (انتهى).]

And it was narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "When the people of Paradise (أهل الجنّة) enter Paradise, some of them will say to others: 'Did our Lord not promise us that we would approach the Fire (النار)?' So He will say to them: 'You have approached it, and it is extinguished (خامدة).'"

It was said: As for the Almighty's saying: أُولئِكَ عَنْها مُبْعَدُونَ ** (those will be kept away from it), the intent is from its torment (عذابها).

And it was said that approaching it (ورودها) means passing over the Bridge (الصّراط), for it is stretched over it.

I say: And all of it is correct, and there is no contradiction between them according to those possessed of understanding (اولي الألباب).

(٧٣) وَإِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِمْ آياتُنا بَيِّناتٍ ** (73) When Our manifest signs are recited to them, (The signs are) whose wordings are recited clearly (مرتّلات الألفاظ), whose meanings are made manifest (مبيّنات المعاني), or whose miraculous nature (الاعجاز) is evident (واضحات) قالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** the faithless say to the faithful, because of them (لأجلهم) or with them (مَعهم) أَيُّ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ ** 'Which of the two parties (meaning) those who believe in them (المؤمنين بها) or those who deny them (الجاحدين لها) خَيْرٌ مَقاماً ** is better in station (meaning) place (مكاناً) or a place of standing (موضع قيام). And it was recited with a dammah on the mīm (مَقاماً to مُقاماً), meaning a place of residence (موضع إقامة) وَأَحْسَنُ نَدِيًّا ** and fairer in assembly?' (meaning) a sitting place (مجلساً) and a gathering place (مجتمعاً). And the meaning is that when they heard the manifest verses (الآيات الواضحات) and were unable to oppose them (معارضتها) or find fault with them (الدّخل عليها), they began to boast about the worldly portions (حظوظ الدّنيا) they possessed, claiming that the increase of their portion therein indicates their superiority (فضلهم) and the goodness of their condition in the sight of Allah.

(٧٤) وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنا قَبْلَهُمْ مِنْ قَرْنٍ هُمْ أَحْسَنُ أَثاثاً ** (74) How many a generation We have destroyed before them, who were better in furnishings furnishings (متاعاً) وَرِءْياً ** and appearance! appearance (مَنْظَراً). And it was recited (رَيّاً) by changing the hamzah (الهمزة) and merging it (إدغامها), or based on it being derived from al-Riyy (الرّيّ) meaning bliss (النّعمة). And it was recited (رِيّاً) based on the change (القلب).

Al-Qummi said: What is meant by it is clothing (الثياب), food (الأكل), and drink (الشّرب).

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Al-Athath (الأثاث) is the furnishings (المتاع), and Ri'yan (رِئْياً) is beauty (الجمال) and a good appearance (المنظر الحسن).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم invited Quraysh to our wilayah (ولايتنا), but they recoiled and denied it. The Almighty's saying: فَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (Then the faithless said) — meaning from Quraysh — لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا الَّذِينَ ** (to the faithful, those who) — meaning those who acknowledged the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and us, the People of the House (أهل البيت) — أَيُّ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ خَيْرٌ مَقاماً وَأَحْسَنُ نَدِيًّا ** (Which of the two parties is better in respect of station and fairer in assembly?) — this being an expression (تَعْبِيراً) from them. So Allah said, in response to them: وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنا قَبْلَهُمْ مِنْ قَرْنٍ ** (How many a generation We have destroyed before them) — meaning from the previous nations (الأُمَمِ السَّابِقَةِ) — the Ayah.

(75) قُلْ مَنْ كانَ فِي الضَّلالَةِ فَلْيَمْدُدْ لَهُ الرَّحْمَنُ مَدًّا (Say, 'As for him who is in error, the All-beneficent will prolong his term considerably') So He prolongs his term and grants him respite with a long life and enjoyment thereof. And He only brought it forth in the form of a command (الأمر) as an indication that His granting of respite (إمهال) is something He ought to do, as a gradual enticement (استدراج) and to cut off his excuses (معاذير), like His saying: إِنَّما نُمْلِي لَهُمْ لِيَزْدادُوا إِثْماً (We only respite them so that they may increase in sin) And His saying: أَوَلَمْ نُعَمِّرْكُمْ ما يَتَذَكَّرُ فِيهِ مَنْ تَذَكَّرَ حَتَّى إِذا رَأَوْا ما يُوعَدُونَ إِمَّا الْعَذابَ وَإِمَّا السَّاعَةَ (Did We not give you a life long enough for whoever was going to take admonition to take admonition in it? But when they see what they are promised—either the punishment or the Hour) This is an elaboration (تفضيل) of what is promised (الموعود). Al-Qummi said: الْعَذابَ (the punishment) is killing (القتل), and السَّاعَةَ (the Hour) is death (الموت). فَسَيَعْلَمُونَ (1) مَنْ هُوَ شَرٌّ مَكاناً (Soon they will know who is worse off in position) from the two parties, by witnessing the matter contrary to what they estimated, and what they enjoyed returned as disappointment (خذلاناً) and a calamity (وبالاً) upon them. وَأَضْعَفُ جُنْداً (and weaker in forces) meaning: party (فئة) and helpers (أنصار). He contrasted this with أَحْسَنُ نَدِيًّا (the best assembly), for the goodness of the assembly (النّد) is due to the gathering of the notables (وجوه القوم) and the manifestation of their might (شوكتهم).

(٧٦) وَيَزِيدُ اللهُ الَّذِينَ اهْتَدَوْا هُدىً (76) And Allah increases those who are guided in guidance.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: "All of them were in error (الضّلالة), not believing in the guardianship (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) nor in our guardianship (ولايتنا). Thus, they were astray (ضالّين) and misleading others (مضلّين). So, He extends for them their error and their transgression (طغيانهم) until they die, and Allah makes them worse in position (شرّاً مكاناً) and weaker in troops (أضعف جنداً)."

He said: "As for His saying: حَتَّى إِذا رَأَوْا ما يُوعَدُونَ ** (until they see what they are promised), it is the advent (خروج) of al-Qa'im (peace be upon him), and that is the Hour (الساعة). They will know on that day what has descended upon them from Allah at the hands of His Qa'im. That is His saying: مَنْ هُوَ شَرٌّ مَكاناً ** (who is worse in position), meaning: in the presence of al-Qa'im, وَأَضْعَفُ جُنْداً وَيَزِيدُ اللهُ ** (and weaker in troops, and Allah increases)—He said: 'He increases them on that day in guidance upon guidance by their following al-Qa'im (peace be upon him), since they neither deny Him (يجحدونه) nor reject Him (ينكرونه).' وَالْباقِياتُ الصَّالِحَاتُ ** (And the lasting good deeds) are the acts of obedience (الطاعات) whose benefit (عائدتها) remains forever and ever (أبداً الآباد). خَيْرٌ عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ ثَواباً ** (are better with your Lord in reward)—meaning: in benefit (عائدة) compared to the deficient (مخدجة) enjoyment (الغانية) of the blessings (النعم) with which the disbelievers were indulged, and with which they boast. وَخَيْرٌ مَرَدًّا ** (and better in return)—meaning: in outcome (مرجعاً وعاقبة). For the outcome of the former is the everlasting bliss (النّعيم المقيم), while the outcome of the latter (the disbelievers' enjoyment) is regret (الحسرة) and perpetual torment (العذاب الدائم). The good deeds (الصالحات) are an explanation (تفسير) of the lasting deeds (الباقيات), and the term 'better' (الخير) here is merely for the sake of increase (الزيادة). Narrations concerning this have previously appeared in Surat al-Kahf.

footnote (١) This is a refutation of their saying: **أَيُّ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ خَيْرٌ مَقاماً وَأَحْسَنُ نَدِيًّا.** (Which of the two parties is better in position and fairer in assembly?)

(٧٧) أَفَرَأَيْتَ الَّذِي كَفَرَ بِآياتِنا وَقالَ لَأُوتَيَنَّ مالاً وَوَلَداً ** (77) Have you seen him who disbelieved in Our signs and said, 'I will surely be given wealth and children'? meaning, in the Hereafter. القمّيّ narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, العاص بن وائل بن هشام القرشيّ ثمّ السهمي—and he was one of the mockers (المستهزئين)—had a right (حقّ) against him owed to خباب بن الأرت. So, he came to him demanding repayment. العاص said to him: "Do you not claim that in Paradise there is gold, silver, and silk?" He said: "Yes, certainly (بلى)." He said: "Then the appointed time (موعد) between me and you is Paradise, for by Allah, I shall surely be given therein better than what I was given in the world."

(78) أَطَّلَعَ الْغَيْبَ ** (Has he peered into the Unseen) His status has reached such greatness that he ascended to the realm of the Unseen (عالم الغيب), which the One, the Subduer (الواحد القهّار), has reserved solely for Himself, until he claimed that he will be given wealth and children in the Hereafter, and he swore an oath concerning it. أَمِ اتَّخَذَ عِنْدَ الرَّحْمنِ عَهْداً ** (Or has he taken a covenant with the All-beneficent?) Or did he take a covenant concerning that from the Knower of the Unseen (علّام الغيوب)? For indeed, knowledge of it cannot be attained except through one of these two ways.

(٧٩) كَلَّا ** (No indeed!) A reprimand (ردع) and an admonition (تنبيه), indicating that he is mistaken in what he imagined for himself. سَنَكْتُبُ ما يَقُولُ وَنَمُدُّ لَهُ مِنَ الْعَذابِ مَدًّا ** (We shall write down what he says, and prolong for him the punishment indefinitely), meaning: and We shall prolong it for him. (٨٠) وَنَرِثُهُ ** (And We shall inherit from him) By Our destruction of him, ما يَقُولُ ** (what he says), meaning the wealth and the children from what he possesses of them both. وَيَأْتِينا ** (and he will come to Us) on the Day of Resurrection فَرْداً ** (alone), neither wealth nor child from what he had in the world will accompany him, let alone that he should be brought there with an increase (زائداً).

(٨١) وَاتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ آلِهَةً لِيَكُونُوا لَهُمْ عِزًّا ** (81) And they have taken gods besides Allah that they may be a source of might for them. So that they may gain might (ليتعزّزوا) through them, since they would be a means (وصلة) for them to Allah and intercessors (شفعاء) with Him. (٨٢) كَلَّا ** (82) Certainly not! A deterrent (ردع) and a denial (إنكار) of their seeking might through them. سَيَكْفُرُونَ بِعِبادَتِهِمْ وَيَكُونُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ ضِدًّا. They will soon disavow their worship, and will be their adversaries. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: Meaning, those whom they took as gods besides Allah will be adversaries (ضدّاً) on the Day of Resurrection, and they will disavow them and their worship. Then He said: "Worship (العبادة) is not merely prostration (السّجود) or bowing (الركوع); rather, it is obedience to men. Whoever obeys a creature in disobedience to the Creator has indeed worshipped him." I say: He (peace be upon him) means by that that the intent of the gods (الآلهة) taken besides Allah are their leaders (رؤساؤهم) whom they obeyed in disobedience to the Creator.

(83) **أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّا أَرْسَلْنَا الشَّياطِينَ عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ تَؤُزُّهُمْ أَزًّا ** (83) Have you not regarded that We sent the devils against the faithless, inciting them with [full] incitement?

They shake them and entice them towards acts of disobedience through insinuations (التَّسْوِيلَات) and making desires beloved.

Al-Qummi said: When they transgressed in it (the world) and in its trial (فِتْنَتِهَا) and in their obedience (to falsehood), and He extended for them their transgression (طُغْيَانِهِمْ) and their misguidance (ضَلَالَتِهِمْ), He sent against them the devils among mankind and the jinn. تَؤُزُّهُمْ أَزًّا ** meaning, they prick them with pricking (نَخْسًا) and urge them towards their obedience and their worship.

(٨٤) فَلا تَعْجَلْ عَلَيْهِمْ إِنَّما نَعُدُّ لَهُمْ عَدًّا ** (84) So do not be in a hurry against them; We are only counting for them a [precise] count. He said: meaning, in their transgression (طغيانهم), their trial (فتنتهم), and their disbelief (كفرهم). I say: And the meaning is: do not hasten their destruction so that you may rest from their evils, for nothing remains for them except counted breaths (أنفاس معدودة). And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He was asked about the Almighty's saying: إِنَّما نَعُدُّ لَهُمْ عَدًّا ** (We are only counting for them a [precise] count). So He said: "What is it, in your view?" The questioner said: "The counting of days (عدد الأيّام)." He said: "Indeed, fathers and mothers count that. No, rather it is the counting of breaths (عدد الأنفاس)." And al-Qummi: the like of it. And in Nahj al-Balagha: The breath (نفس) of a person is his step (خطاؤه) towards his term (أجله). And He (peace be upon him) said: "Every counted thing is diminished (منقصّ), and every awaited thing is coming (آت)."
