(٨٥) يَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ الْمُتَّقِينَ إِلَى الرَّحْمَنِ وَفْدًا (85) The day We shall gather the Godwary to the All-beneficent as a delegation, The Almighty's saying: يَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ الْمُتَّقِينَ (The day We shall gather the Godwary) meaning: We shall gather them إِلَى الرَّحْمَنِ (to the All-beneficent) meaning: to their Lord, Who encompassed them with His mercy وَفْدًا (as a delegation) meaning: arriving before Him, just as delegations (الوفّاد) arrive before kings, awaiting their honor and bounties. (٨٦) وَنَسُوقُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ إِلَى جَهَنَّمَ وِرْدًا (86) and drive the guilty to hell, thirsty. And the Almighty's saying: وَنَسُوقُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ (and drive the guilty) meaning: just as livestock are driven إِلى جَهَنَّمَ وِرْدًا (to hell, thirsty) meaning: thirsty (عطاشاً), for none approaches water except due to thirst, or like the beasts that approach water. And in the recitation (قراءة) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, narrated from the people of the Household (أهل البيت) عليهم السلام: يوم يحشر المتّقون الى الرّحمن وفدا ويساق المجرمون الى جهنّم ورداً (The day the Godwary are gathered to the All-beneficent as a delegation, and the guilty are driven to Hell, thirsty). This was heard thus from the grave of al-Rida عليه السلام, and its story is mentioned in al-Uyun. In al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: They will be gathered upon noble mounts (النجائب). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, and in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: Ali عليه السلام asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله concerning the interpretation of the Almighty's saying: يَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ الْمُتَّقِينَ the Ayah. He said: "O Ali, a delegation (الوفد) is only composed of riders (ركباناً). Those are men who feared Allah, so Allah loved them, chose them, and was pleased with their deeds, thus naming them the Godwary (المتّقين)." Then He said: "O Ali, by Him Who split the seed and created the soul (النّسمة), they shall surely emerge from their graves, and the angels shall receive them with she-camels (نوق) of glory, upon them saddles of gold, adorned with pearls (الدّر) and rubies (الياقوت). Their coverings (جلالها) shall be brocade (الإستبرق) and fine silk (السّندس), their headstalls (خطامها) shall be twisted purple silk (جدل الأرجوان), and their reins (زمامها) shall be of chrysolite (زبرجد). They will fly with them to the gathering place (المحشر). With every man among them shall be a thousand angels, from before him, and from his right, and from his left, escorting them gently (يزفّونهم زفّاً) until they reach the Greatest Gate of Paradise. At the Gate of Paradise is a tree, the shade of one of its leaves covers a hundred thousand people. To the right of the tree is a purified, sanctified spring (عين مطهّرة مزكّية)." He said: "They will be given a drink from it, sip by sip, and Allah will purify their hearts of envy (الحسد) through it, and the hair will fall from their skins (أبشارهم). That is His saying: وَسَقاهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ شَرَابًا طَهُورًا (and their Lord will give them a purifying drink) [76:21] from that purified spring. Then they will turn to another spring to the left of the tree and bathe in it, and it is the Spring of Life (عين الحياة), so they will never die." Then He said: "They will be made to stand before the Throne, having been saved forever from afflictions (الآفات), diseases (الاسقام), heat, and cold." He said: "Then the Almighty (الجبّار) will say to the angels who are with them: 'Gather My friends (أوليائي) into Paradise, and do not make them stand with the rest of creation (الخلائق), for My pleasure with them has preceded, and My mercy for them has become incumbent. How can I desire to make them stand with the people of good deeds (الحسنات) and evil deeds (السّيئات)?'" He said: "So the angels will drive them to Paradise. When they reach the Greatest Gate of Paradise, the angels will strike the ring (الحلقة) a single strike, and it will produce a ringing sound (صريراً). The sound of its ringing will reach every Houri whom Allah created and prepared for His friends. They will rejoice at their arrival when they hear the ringing of the ring, and some will say to others: 'The friends of Allah have come to us.' Then the gate will open for them, and they will enter Paradise. Their spouses, both the wide-eyed Houri and the human women (الآدميين), will look down upon them and say: 'Welcome to you! How intense was our longing for you!' And the friends of Allah will say the same to them." And al-Qummi added: Ali عليه السلام said: "Who are these, O Messenger of Allah?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "These are your followers (شيعتك), O Ali, and you are their Imam. And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: يَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ الْمُتَّقِينَ إِلَى الرَّحْمَنِ وَفْدًا (The day We shall gather the Godwary to the All-beneficent as a delegation) upon riding beasts (على الرّحايل)."

(87) لا يَمْلِكُونَ الشَّفاعَةَ إِلَّا مَنِ اتَّخَذَ عِنْدَ الرَّحْمنِ عَهْداً (They will not have any power of intercession, except him who has taken a covenant with the All-beneficent.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Except him who submitted to Allah through the wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and the Imams (peace be upon them) after him. That is the covenant (العهد) with Allah."

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "They will not intercede for them, nor will they intercede, إِلَّا مَنِ اتَّخَذَ عِنْدَ الرَّحْمنِ عَهْداً ** (except him who has taken a covenant with the All-beneficent), except him who was granted permission through the wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful and the Imams (peace be upon them) after him. That is the covenant (العهد) with Allah."

In al-Kafi, al-Faqih, al-Tahdhib, and al-Qummi, narrated from him (al-Sadiq) from his father, from his forefathers (peace be upon them), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Whoever does not perfect his will (وصيّته) at the time of his death, there is a deficiency in his chivalry (مروّته)." It was asked: "O Messenger of Allah, and how does one make a will (يوصى) at the time of death?" He said: "When death approaches him and people gather around him, he says: 'O Allah, Originator of the heavens and the earth, Knower of the unseen and the manifest, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful, I covenant with You in the abode of this world that I bear witness that there is no god except You, alone, without any partner, and that Muhammad is Your servant and Your Messenger, and that Paradise is true (حقّ), and that the Fire is true, and that the Resurrection is true, and the Reckoning is true, and the Decree (القدر) is true, and the Balance (الميزان) is true, and that the Religion (الدّين) is as You described, and that Islam is as You legislated, and that the saying (القول) is as You narrated, and that the Quran is as You revealed, and that You are Allah, the True, the Manifest (المبين). May Allah reward Muhammad on our behalf with the best reward, and may Allah greet Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad with peace. O Allah, O my provision (عدّتي) in my distress, O my companion in my hardship, O my guardian in my blessing, my God and the God of my forefathers, do not entrust me to myself for the blink of an eye, ever. For if You entrust me to myself for the blink of an eye, I would be closer to evil and further from good. So, comfort my loneliness (وحشتي) in the grave, and make for me a covenant (عهداً) published (منشوراً) on the Day I meet You.' Then he makes a will concerning his need." And the confirmation of this will (الوصيّة) is in Surah Maryam (peace be upon her) in His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: لا يَمْلِكُونَ الشَّفاعَةَ إِلَّا مَنِ اتَّخَذَ عِنْدَ الرَّحْمنِ عَهْداً ** (They will not have any power of intercession, except him who has taken a covenant with the All-beneficent.) This is the covenant (عهد) of the deceased, and the will (الوصيّة) is an obligation (حقّ) upon every Muslim, and it is incumbent upon him to preserve this will and learn it. And Ali (peace be upon him) said: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله taught it to me, and he said: 'Jibril (peace be upon him) taught it to me.'"

And in al-Jawami', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: That He said to His companions one day: "Is any one of you incapable of taking a covenant (عهداً) with Allah every morning and evening?" They said: "How is that?" He said: "He says: 'O Allah, Originator of the heavens and the earth, and Knower of the unseen and the manifest, I covenant with You that I bear witness that there is no god except You, alone, without any partner, and that Muhammad is Your servant and Your Messenger, and that if You entrust me to myself, You bring me closer to evil and distance me from good, and I trust in nothing except Your mercy. So, make for me a covenant (عهداً) with You which You will fulfill for me on the Day of Resurrection, for You do not break the promise (الميعاد).'" When he says that, it is sealed with a seal (طابع) and placed beneath the Throne (العرش). When the Day of Resurrection comes, a caller will cry out: "Where are those who have a covenant (عهد) with Allah?" So they will enter Paradise.

(٨٨) وَقالُوا اتَّخَذَ الرَّحْمنُ وَلَداً ** (88) And they say, 'The All-beneficent has taken a son.' And it was recited (وُلدا), which is the plural of (وَلَد). Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: This is where Quraysh said that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, had taken a son from the female angels. (٨٩) لَقَدْ جِئْتُمْ شَيْئاً إِدًّا ** (89) Certainly you have brought a monstrous thing! He said: meaning, a great (عظيماً) thing. (٩٠) تَكادُ السَّماواتُ ** (90) The heavens are almost rent apart for it, And it was recited with the (ياء) [as (يَكادُ)] يَتَفَطَّرْنَ مِنْهُ ** (yatafaṭṭarna minhu). And it was recited (يَنفَطِرنَ مِنهُ) (yanfaṭirna minhu). He said: meaning, because of what they said and what they accused Him of. وَتَنْشَقُّ الْأَرْضُ وَتَخِرُّ الْجِبالُ هَدًّا ** and the earth splits open, and the mountains fall down in ruins, Meaning, demolished (مهدودة), broken (مكسورة), or they are demolished with a demolition (هدّاً), or they fall down for the sake of demolition (للْهَدّ) because of what they said.

(٩١) أَنْ دَعَوْا لِلرَّحْمنِ وَلَداً (91) That they ascribe a son to the All-beneficent. (٩٢) وَما يَنْبَغِي لِلرَّحْمنِ أَنْ يَتَّخِذَ وَلَداً ** (92) It is not for the All-beneficent to take a son. It is not fitting for Him, nor is it appropriate for Him; if it were sought, it would be impossible (لِاسْتِحَالَتِهِ), for اِبْتَغَى (to seek/take) is the passive equivalent (مُطَاوِع) of بَغَى (to desire/seek). (٩٣) إِنْ كُلُّ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ إِلَّا آتِي الرَّحْمنِ عَبْداً ** (93) There is none in the heavens and the earth but comes to the All-beneficent as a servant. He resorts to Him through servitude (العبودية) and submission (الانقياد). He does not claim for himself what these people claim. (٩٤) لَقَدْ أَحْصاهُمْ ** (94) Certainly He has counted them He confined them and encompassed them, such that they do not escape the domain (حوزة) of His knowledge and the grasp (قبضة) of His power. وَ عَدَّهُمْ عَدًّا and numbered them precisely. He counted their persons (أشخاصهم), their breaths (أنفاسهم), and their deeds (أفعالهم), for everything with Him is by measure (بمقدار). (٩٥) وَكُلُّهُمْ آتِيهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ فَرْداً (95) And each of them will come to Him on the Day of Resurrection alone. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: "One by one (واحداً واحداً)." It is said that perhaps the arrangement of the ruling with the attribute of All-beneficence (الرّحمانية) is to indicate that everything besides Him is a blessing (نعمة) and one who is blessed (منعم عليه). Thus, it does not share the nature (يجانس) of the One Who is the Originator (مبدأ) of all blessings and the Master (مولى) of their fundamentals (أصولها) and branches (فروعها). So how can He possibly take a son? Al-Qummi narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) that He said: "The trees had always been entirely soft (خضيداً) until [the claim was made] that the All-beneficent (exalted is the All-beneficent and majestic is He) had a son. Then the heavens almost يَتَفَطَّرْنَ مِنْهُ وَتَنْشَقُّ الْأَرْضُ وَتَخِرُّ الْجِبالُ هَدًّا ** split open because of it, and the earth cracked, and the mountains fell down in ruins. At that point, the trees shuddered (اقشعرّ) and grew thorns (شوك), fearing that the punishment (العذاب) might descend upon them."

(٩٦) **إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ سَيَجْعَلُ لَهُمُ الرَّحْمنُ وُدًّا ** (96) Indeed, those who have faith and do righteous deeds—the All-beneficent will vest love in them.

He will cause affection (مودّة) for them in the hearts.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: The reason for the revelation of this verse was that the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) was sitting before the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny). So he [the Messenger] said to him: "Say, O Ali: 'O Allah, place affection (ودّاً) for me in the hearts of the believers.'" Then Allah revealed [the verse].

And Al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) supplicated for the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) at the end of his prayers, raising his voice so that the people could hear, saying: "O Allah, grant Ali (peace be upon him) affection (المودّة) in the breasts of the believers, and awe (الهيبة) and greatness (العظمة) in the breasts of the hypocrites." Then Allah revealed the verse: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (Indeed, those who have faith...).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) concerning this verse: A similar narration.

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) said to Ali (peace be upon him): "Say: 'O Allah, establish a covenant (عَهداً) for me with You, and place affection (ودّا) for me in the hearts of the believers.'" So he said both of them, and this verse was revealed.

(٩٧) فَإِنَّما يَسَّرْناهُ بِلِسانِكَ by having sent it down in your language لِتُبَشِّرَ بِهِ الْمُتَّقِينَ وَتُنْذِرَ بِهِ قَوْماً لُدًّا ** (97) We have only made it easy in your language, that you may give good news thereby to the Godwary, and warn thereby a contentious lot. those severe in contention (الخصومة).

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): فَإِنَّما يَسَّرْناهُ ** means the Quran. Regarding قَوْماً لُدًّا , He said: "The people of al-Kalam (الكلام) and contention (الخصومة)."

And in Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Regarding His saying إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا , He said: "It is Ali عليه السلام." Regarding قَوْماً لُدًّا , He said: "Banu Umayyah (بني أميّة), a tyrannical people (قوماً ظلمة)."

And in al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, He said: "Allah only made it easy upon his tongue when the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام established a standard (علماً). So, He gave good news thereby to the believers and warned thereby the disbelievers, and they are those whom Allah mentioned in His Book as لُدًّا , meaning disbelievers (كفّاراً)."

(٩٨) وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنا قَبْلَهُمْ مِنْ قَرْنٍ ** (98) How many a generation We have destroyed before them! This is an intimidation for the disbelievers and an emboldening of the Messenger (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) to warn them. هَلْ تُحِسُّ مِنْهُمْ مِنْ أَحَدٍ ** Do you perceive anyone of them, Do you sense anyone of them and see him, أَوْ تَسْمَعُ لَهُمْ رِكْزاً or hear a whisper from them? The Rikz (الرّكز) is the faint sound.

Al-Qummi narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: Allah destroyed nations that you cannot count. Then He said, "O Muhammad, هَلْ تُحِسُّ مِنْهُمْ مِنْ أَحَدٍ أَوْ تَسْمَعُ لَهُمْ رِكْزاً. (Do you perceive anyone of them, or hear a whisper from them?)"

In Thawāb al-A'māl and al-Majma', narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Whoever persists in reciting Surah Maryam (peace be upon her) will not die until he attains that which suffices him regarding himself, his wealth, and his children. And in the Hereafter, he will be among the companions of Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon our Prophet and his Progeny, and upon them both be peace and blessings), and he will be granted authority (من الأمر) like the kingdom of Solomon son of David (peace be upon him) in this world.

SURAH TAHA

Makkan, its number of verses is one hundred and thirty-five verses (Shami), and thirty (Kufi), and four (Hijazi), and two (Basri). بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)
